narrative: Battery chemistry changed LiPo→LiFePO4: thermal event KES-208 triggered design review WIKI-052 where Dr. Rhea Voss decided to switch; ECR-214 raised by Lena Okafor, approved ECN-312; DOC-421 updated to Rev C; P-1062 cell assembly and P-1075 BMS updated.
key_facts: LiPo thermal runaway occurred 8 Sep 2023 during hot-day endurance test
Dr. Rhea Voss made the decision at WIKI-052 on 2023-10-05
Rationale: IEC 62133-2 compliance requirement and second thermal event
Energy density reduced ~12% but 66min endurance still exceeds 60min minimum
ECN-312 approved 2023-10-19

narrative: Propulsion motors replaced: thermal cutout (KES-187) and ESC overcurrent (KES-188) in April 2024 triggered WIKI-031 emergency review; Marcus Thaine and Dr. Voss decided to replace P-1031/P-1032 with P-1041/P-1042; ECR-221 raised, ECN-318 approved; DOC-445 qualification test report created.
key_facts: P-1031 winding temperature reached 108°C (rated 85°C max) at 30°C ambient
Customer requirement specifies 40°C maximum ambient operating temperature
Root cause: insufficient stator-to-case thermal conductivity in 4012 490KV winding
P-1041 (4014 420KV Enhanced) reduces stator temp by ~18°C at same operating point
Decision made at emergency review WIKI-031, 2024-04-10

narrative: GNC sensor suite upgraded: flight test DOC-467 showed P-1092 IMU attitude errors 2.1° (requirement 0.5°) due to motor vibration harmonics; WIKI-067 design review agreed replacement with ADIS16505-2 (P-1093) and helical GPS antenna (P-1098); ECR-228/ECN-326; SRC-007 and SRC-009 updated; DOC-455 updated to Rev B.
key_facts: ICM-42688-P (P-1092) resonant frequency 4.8kHz coincides with P-1041 motor harmonics
Attitude error 2.1° roll measured in flight tests (requirement: 0.5°)
ADIS16505-2 (P-1093) has 18kHz resonant frequency, 8x better vibration rectification error
Design review at WIKI-067, 2024-12-03
SRC-007 nav_filter.c EKF driver updated for ADIS SPI interface

narrative: Sonar transducer vendor changed: DepthWave frequency drift (KES-158) during testing (KES-156) was unacceptable; WIKI-045 vendor selection meeting chose AquaSound Corp; ECR-218/ECN-315; DOC-462 updated; SRC-011 DSP filter updated.
key_facts: DepthWave centre frequency drifted 200kHz→184kHz (16kHz) over 2-hour thermal soak at 35°C
SRC-011 sonar_driver DSP filter tuned to 200kHz ±2kHz — cannot compensate 16kHz drift
AquaSound Corp selected: ±1.5kHz stability, 100–400kHz bandwidth, 30m depth rating, DNV-GL certified
Vendor selection at WIKI-045, 2024-08-29; ECR-218 raised by Cass Doyle
O-ring flange compatibility with P-1149 bracket confirmed at WIKI-045

narrative: Comms encryption upgraded: penetration test found command injection vulnerability; customer contract (KES-203) required AES-256-GCM by M4; WIKI-071 review agreed P-1127 FPGA module and radio upgrade P-1125→P-1126; ECR-229/ECN-322; SRC-018 and SRC-024 updated; DOC-471 updated to Rev C.
key_facts: Penetration test MD-SEC-2024-11 (Dec 2024) found command injection vulnerability on unencrypted P-1125 link
Customer contract requires AES-256-GCM by milestone M4 (March 2025)
P-1126 (2Mbps) replaces P-1125 (500kbps) to accommodate 3% AES overhead
P-1127 FPGA module (Lattice iCE40, AES-256-GCM) added inline between P-1121 and radio
SRC-018 encryption_handler.c implements FPGA interface; SRC-024 handles GCS session

narrative: Wing skin material changed CFRP→hybrid carbon-aramid: delamination at wing root (KES-045) found during ground vibration test; WIKI-017 review; ECR-207/ECN-307; DOC-409 structural spec and DOC-407 loads analysis updated; DRW-502 updated.
key_facts: CFRP (0°/90°) wing skins delaminated at wing root bond line after 12h of motor vibration
Hybrid carbon-aramid (CFRP + Kevlar® ±45° interlayer) passes 100h with no delamination
Mass penalty +50g total, within 340g system margin
Stiffness 8% lower than CFRP — loads re-run in DOC-407, flutter onset still >45m/s
Decision at WIKI-017, 2023-08-22; Dr. Voss approved

narrative: GCS software architecture refactored: monolithic SRC-001 (8400 lines) caused UI thread blocking during AES encryption integration; WIKI-058 agreed asyncio backend/frontend split; ECR-225/ECN-320; SRC-024 created; DOC-479 written.
key_facts: SRC-001 gcs_main.py grew to 8,400 lines with synchronous event loop
Encryption integration (KES-201, P-1127 FPGA) caused 800ms–2s UI freezes in v1
v2 uses asyncio Python backend (SRC-024) with WebSocket API to Vue.js frontend
SRC-024 calls SRC-018 (encryption_handler.c) via Python ctypes
Design review at WIKI-058, 2024-10-15

narrative: Camera gimbal redesigned: GimbalTech 2-axis unit (KES-137) had 8.2Hz resonance coinciding with airframe wing flutter mode (DOC-407 8–9Hz); WIKI-038 review; ECR-216/ECN-313; new P-1154 Rev C 3-axis gimbal with P-1158 controller; SRC-021 and DOC-449 updated.
key_facts: GimbalTech resonant frequency 8.2Hz coincided with airframe wing flutter mode at 8–9Hz
Image jitter ±4° peak-to-peak at resonance (requirement: ±0.5°)
New P-1154 Rev C frame natural frequency 24Hz — 3x above airframe flutter mode
P-1158 SimpleBGC 32-bit controller with independent onboard IMU
Qualification flight test achieved ±0.09° jitter

narrative: Battery cell (P-1062) → BMS module (P-1075) → BMS monitor code (SRC-003) → Power Budget ICD (DOC-415) → Jira compliance story (KES-112): the LiFePO4 cell assembly's voltage characteristics drive BMS profiles, which SRC-003 monitors, feeding the power budget model documented in DOC-415.
key_facts: P-1062 LiFePO4 cells have 3.2V nominal vs 3.7V LiPo — BMS profiles must match
P-1075 BMS hardware lockout enforces 18%/82% SOC limits (from ECR-209/REV-623)
SRC-003 bms_monitor.py reads BMS state and feeds power manager
DOC-415 Power Budget ICD references P-1062 energy capacity and P-1075 limits

narrative: IMU module (P-1093) → nav filter code (SRC-007) → GNC ICD (DOC-455) → flight test report (DOC-467) → GNC upgrade epic (KES-089): the tactical IMU's sensor data feeds the EKF in SRC-007, whose performance is specified in DOC-455 and validated in DOC-467.
key_facts: P-1093 ADIS16505-2 connects via SPI to FCU (P-1091); driver in SRC-007
SRC-007 EKF fuses IMU with GPS (P-1094), barometer (P-1095), magnetometer (P-1096)
DOC-455 specifies 0.5° attitude accuracy requirement — met by P-1093 + SRC-007
DOC-467 flight test report validates 0.28° roll in-flight

narrative: Sonar transducer (P-1148) → sonar driver (SRC-011) → sonar ICD (DOC-462) → sonar test report (DOC-468) → sonar integration story (KES-156): the transducer's frequency characteristics drive the DSP filter in SRC-011, defined in DOC-462 and validated in DOC-468.
key_facts: P-1148 AquaSound Corp, 200kHz centre, ±1.5kHz stability
SRC-011 sonar_driver.c DSP filter centre 200kHz ±2kHz (narrowed in Rev B for AquaSound)
DOC-462 §6.2 documents the critical 7° cant angle on P-1149 bracket
DOC-468 sonar performance test report validates AquaSound supplier qualification

narrative: Radio transceiver (P-1126) → encryption handler (SRC-018) → Comms ICD (DOC-471) → Protocol Spec (DOC-472) → comms encryption epic (KES-201): the dual-band radio feeds the AES-256 FPGA (P-1127), whose protocol is specified in DOC-471/DOC-472.
key_facts: P-1126 (2Mbps) required because AES-256-GCM adds ~3% overhead over P-1125 (500kbps)
SRC-018 encryption_handler.c interfaces the P-1127 FPGA via SPI from P-1121
DOC-471 Rev C documents the encrypted framing; DOC-472 the session protocol
GCS side: SRC-024 gcs_ui_backend.py calls SRC-018 via ctypes

narrative: Motor P-1041 → motor/ESC controller P-1044 → motor qualification test (DOC-445) → airframe loads (DOC-407) → motor mount (P-1012): the enhanced motor drives thermal requirements on the controller board and structural requirements on the motor mount.
key_facts: P-1041 4014 420KV enhanced motor replaces P-1031 per ECN-318
P-1044 motor/ESC controller board — CAN bus, STM32, compatible with P-1041
DOC-445 qualification test: P-1041 peaks at 79°C at 40°C ambient (rated 85°C max)
P-1012 motor mounts — fit compatibility confirmed at WIKI-031

narrative: Camera gimbal (P-1154) → gimbal controller (P-1158) → gimbal driver (SRC-021) → Payload ICD (DOC-449) → camera ICD appendix (DOC-480): the 3-axis gimbal assembly feeds stabilised video through the controller board to the GCS payload display.
key_facts: P-1154 Rev C 3-axis gimbal replaces GimbalTech per ECN-313
P-1158 SimpleBGC 32-bit extended — serial API interface documented in SRC-021
SRC-021 gimbal_driver.py updated for P-1158 serial protocol (not SimpleBGC 8-bit)
DOC-449 §3 specifies gimbal interface and ±0.5° jitter requirement

narrative: GPS antenna (P-1098) → GPS driver (SRC-009) → GPS performance spec (DOC-456) → flight log schema (DOC-481) → GPS epic sub-story (KES-098): the helical antenna's L5 support feeds position data via SRC-009 into the flight log schema defined in DOC-481.
key_facts: P-1098 helical antenna adds L5 band support vs P-1097 patch (L1/L2 only)
SRC-009 gps_driver.c updated for L5 support per ECN-326
DOC-456 GPS Performance Specification updated to Rev B for P-1098 characteristics
Near-hemispherical coverage of P-1098 eliminates dropout at roll >15°

narrative: Battery wiring harness (P-1079) → airframe wiring bracket (P-1017) → battery bay drawing (DRW-503) → airframe integration ICD (DOC-413) → airframe loads (DOC-407): the power harness routing through the airframe structure creates mechanical dependencies through brackets to structural docs.
key_facts: P-1079 battery wiring harness routes from P-1061 enclosure via P-1017 bracket to P-1076 PDB
P-1017 airframe wiring bracket is bonded to P-1001 fuselage main body
DRW-503 battery bay assembly drawing shows harness routing
DOC-413 airframe integration ICD defines all harness attachment points

narrative: IMU change (ECR-228) impact across 9 artifacts: obsoletes P-1092, introduces P-1093; requires SRC-007 EKF driver update; requires SRC-009 GPS driver update (bundled); updates DOC-455 GNC ICD, DOC-456 GPS spec, DOC-467 flight test report; updates DRW-526 and DRW-538 installation drawings.
key_facts: 10+ artifacts impacted by ECR-228
SRC-007 nav_filter.c requires IMU driver rewrite (SPI interface, different register map)
DOC-455, DOC-456, DOC-467 all require update
DRW-526 (IMU & Baro Installation) and DRW-538 (IMU v2 Installation) both updated
P-1103 IMU vibration damper — compatible with P-1093 form factor, no change

narrative: Battery voltage spec change (ECR-214 LiPo→LiFePO4) impact: P-1062 cell chemistry, P-1075 BMS profiles, P-1044 motor ESC bus voltage compatibility check, P-1079 harness no change, DOC-421 full rewrite, DOC-415 power budget update, SRC-003 BMS monitor update, all linked to KES-208.
key_facts: LiFePO4 nominal cell voltage 3.2V vs LiPo 3.7V — pack voltage 19.2V vs 22.2V nominal
P-1075 BMS charge/discharge profiles must be rewritten for LiFePO4 voltage curves
P-1044 motor controller board voltage input range confirmed compatible (15–28V)
SRC-003 bms_monitor.py SOC thresholds updated for LiFePO4 OCV curve
DOC-421 fully rewritten to Rev C

narrative: Sonar frequency change (ECR-218 vendor change) impact: P-1148 transducer updated, SRC-011 DSP filter updated, DOC-462 ICD updated, DOC-468 test report reissued, DRW-517 notes updated, P-1149 mounting bracket O-ring check required.
key_facts: AquaSound Corp uses identical centre frequency (200kHz) — DSP centre unchanged
Anti-alias filter width narrowed from ±20kHz to ±2kHz for AquaSound's tighter stability
O-ring groove dimensions match (confirmed at WIKI-045)
DRW-517 notes updated to document 7° cant angle rationale (per REV-641)
DOC-468 sonar performance test report reissued with AquaSound supplier data

narrative: Wing skin material change (ECR-207) impact: P-1008 and P-1009 updated to hybrid composite, DOC-409 structural spec updated, DOC-407 loads analysis re-run (stiffness -8%, flutter still OK), DRW-502 layup schedule updated, +50g mass impact reducing system margin.
key_facts: Both wing skins (P-1008 port, P-1009 starboard) updated simultaneously
Stiffness 8% lower — flutter onset now 52m/s (requirement >45m/s) — still PASS
Mass +50g total reduces system margin from 340g to 290g
P-1017 airframe wiring bracket bonded to fuselage, not wing skin — no change
DRW-502 updated with CFRP + aramid ±45° + CFRP layup schedule

narrative: Comms bandwidth increase (ECR-229) impact: P-1125 obsoleted, P-1126 introduced, P-1127 FPGA encryption module added, P-1167 GCS radio updated, SRC-018 and SRC-024 updated, DOC-471 and DOC-472 updated, DRW-531 updated.
key_facts: 9 artifacts directly impacted by ECR-229
P-1167 GCS Radio Unit must be swapped to FreeWave GXM (paired with P-1126)
DRW-531 enhanced antenna mount updated for P-1126 different form factor
GCS antennas (P-1165, P-1166) do not change — dual-band radios are backward compatible
Security clearance from customer for encryption design: MD-SEC-2025-01

narrative: GPS module change (ECR-228, bundled with IMU change) impact: P-1097 obsoleted, P-1098 helical antenna introduced, SRC-009 gps_driver.c updated for L5 band, DOC-456 GPS spec updated, DOC-467 flight test re-run, DRW-534 installation drawing updated.
key_facts: P-1098 has different form factor (helical vs patch) — DRW-534 must be updated
P-1102 GPS antenna mount — updated design for helical form factor
L5 band support in SRC-009: additional signal tracking channels enabled
DOC-456 updated to reflect L5 band, improved C/N0 at low elevation angles
DOC-467 re-run confirms GPS reliability at roll angles >15° — no more dropouts

narrative: BMS fail-safe thresholds — the 18%/82% SOC limits on P-1075 (BMS Module) were set by Dr. Rhea Voss (departed Nov 2024) based on March 2023 bench tests. The full rationale exists ONLY in REV-623 (Voss's review comments on DOC-431) and WIKI-014 (BMS Safety Architecture Review). The DOC-431 itself references but does not fully explain the rationale — REV-623 is the primary source.
key_facts: Dr. Rhea Voss departed 2024-11-15 — she is no longer available for questions
18% SOC floor prevents cell reversal onset (observed at 12% in BT-2023-03-12 bench test)
82% SOC ceiling prevents lithium plating (observed at >85% SOC below 5°C)
Both limits implemented in HARDWARE on P-1075 to prevent single-point software fault bypass
Key rationale is in REV-623 comments C01 and C02 — not summarised in any other artifact

narrative: System architecture Level 3 autonomy decision — Dr. Rhea Voss (departed Nov 2024) chose SAE Level 3 (not Level 4) in WIKI-003 kick-off meeting, driven by customer RFT-K200-002. The rationale and the constraint on operations near infrastructure are in REV-631 (Voss review of DOC-401) — not in DOC-401 itself or ECN-301.
key_facts: Dr. Rhea Voss departed 2024-11-15
Level 3 chosen because customer RFT-K200-002 prohibits Level 4 in mixed-traffic maritime zones
Single-string GNC was a deliberate mass trade (redundant FCU adds ~300g, exceeds 8kg MTOW)
K-200 is NOT safe for urban or littoral operations with current architecture
These constraints are in REV-631 comments C01 and C02 — not fully stated in DOC-401

narrative: Sonar mounting angle rationale — the 7° downward cant on P-1149 bracket (DRW-517) was explained ONLY by Cass Doyle (departed Mar 2025) in REV-641 review of DRW-517. At 0° cant the transducer null zone coincides with minimum operating depth. The rationale is not in DOC-462 main body but is cross-referenced from there.
key_facts: Cass Doyle departed 2025-03-07 — no longer available
At 0° cant, transducer near-field null zone (~0.45m) coincides with minimum ops depth (0.5m)
7° cant shifts null zone to ~1.2m depth, clearing the entire operational envelope
Validated in November 2024 pool tests at 0.5m, 1.0m, and 2.0m depth (KES-156)
Angle is fixed in hardware (P-1149 bracket geometry) — software offset cannot fix this
REV-641 C01 is the only place the full rationale is documented
